title: Let containers/storage keep track of mounts
body:
Currently we unmount storage that is still in use.
We should not be unmounting storeage that we mounted
via a different command or by podman mount.  This
change relies on containers/storage to umount keep track of
how many times the storage was mounted before really unmounting
it from the system.

Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

comments.body: I think we still want to use c.state.Mounted for most container operations that aren't Mount() and Unmount() - we call c.prepare() a lot, which calls the mount code, and it would be bad if each call added a mount we have to undo to actually unmount. We might want to split mount handling for Mount() and Unmount() away from the code we use internally to maintain the split.
Yes I agree.  I will look into that. I am trying to get container/storage better updated so libpod would be able to tell what is going on.

One potential problem - I think `Unmount()` needs to be able to set `c.state.Mounted` to false if it really unmounts in c/storage, to make sure that if someone calls `podman unmount` too many times and unmounts the mount we created with `c.prepare()` as the container started. Ideally, we'd have something where `podman unmount` could only unmount as much as `podman mount` had been called, so we could avoid that.
I added mounted to container storage which returns whether or not it is mounted, I could change it to specify how many times, then if it is 1 and ctr.state.Mounted = True, I could refuse to Unmount.
That makes a lot of sense. SGTM.
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably 98703eb) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.

LGTM, this is miles better than what we have now - thanks @rhatdan
